lines: okay the animal town one, made up
in Hollow Creek the animals all had little shops
Mrs. Otter ran the bakery and Buckley the badger fixed boots
and one morning the creek ran dry, no water, all the way up
uh oh how do they fix it
the young squirrel Fenn went up the hill and found a beaver dam too big
and instead of fighting the beavers, he helped them build a little gate in it
so the dam stayed and the creek flowed, everybody wins
and they had a pie party, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal village (Hollow Creek, Fenn); invented, not Redwall/Paw Patrol.

lines: the mouse mail carrier, you'll like this
a little mouse named Pib carried letters all over the meadow
in a backpack bigger than she was
and a storm knocked all the signposts down, so nobody knew the way
and the letters had to get to the sick old turtle who was waiting
so Pib followed her nose and the smell of his lily pond
and got there soaked but smiling with a whole bag of get-well cards
and the turtle felt better just from that, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Pib the mouse); invented, not a franchise.

lines: the hedgehog who was afraid of his own prickles
his name was Quill and he never hugged anybody, scared he'd poke them
so he was the loneliest hedgehog in Brook Hollow
aw
but then the bunnies got cold in winter and had no blanket
and Quill rolled in the dry leaves till he was a big soft leaf-ball
and the bunnies snuggled right up against the leaves, no pokes
and Quill finally got his hugs, the end, goodnight
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Quill, Brook Hollow); invented, no franchise.

lines: the frog choir of Lily Bottom
every night the frogs sang the pond to sleep
but one frog, little Croak, had a tiny squeaky voice and was embarrassed
so he just mouthed the words
and one night the big frogs all lost their voices from a cold
and the pond couldn't sleep, all cranky
so Croak sang alone, squeaky and brave, and it turned out squeaky was perfect for a lullaby
and after that he always got the last song, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Croak, Lily Bottom); invented, no franchise.

lines: okay the fox librarian
there was a fox named Ember who kept a library in a hollow oak
all the books were stories the animals told her, she wrote them down
and a little wren couldn't sleep cause she had no story of her own
so Ember said let's make yours right now
and they wrote the wren a story about a brave bird who flew over the mountain
and the wren fell asleep before they even finished it
and Ember put it on the shelf, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Ember the fox); invented, no franchise.

lines: the snail and tortoise race, but not how you think
in Mossgate the animals had a yearly race
and a snail named Sol and a tortoise named Plod were always dead last
every year, last, last
so one year they decided to race each other, slow as they liked, all day
and they talked the whole way and watched the clouds
and they crossed the line together at sunset, holding the same ribbon
and they said that was the best race ever, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Mossgate, Sol and Plod); invented, no franchise.

lines: the owl who collected lost feathers
old Professor Hoom lived in the tallest pine
and after every storm he gathered the feathers the birds dropped
why
cause come winter he sewed them into the warmest quilt in the forest
and any animal who was cold could come sleep under it
and one night a shivering little sparrow knocked
and Hoom tucked her right in, under a hundred soft feathers, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Professor Hoom the owl); invented, no franchise.

lines: okay the raccoon who washed the moon
in Tangle Wood a raccoon named Pockets washed everything in the stream
berries, pebbles, his own little hands, everything
and one night the moon's reflection looked dusty to him
so he tried to wash the moon, splash splash in the water
haha you can't wash the moon
the other animals laughed, but the splashing made the water sparkle so pretty
that they all came down to watch, and it became the nightly moon-washing party, the end
stories.tell_note: Original talking-animal (Pockets, Tangle Wood); invented, no franchise.
